title: Roche FastStart Taq PCR
description: Roche FastStart Taq DNA Polymerase (5 U/uL): hot-start 50 uL reaction with a 95 C / 4 min activation step and 2 mM MgCl2 standard.

ingredients.name: MgCl2 25 mM Stock (Vial 4, optional)
ingredients.concentration: for Mg titration to 1.5-4 mM final

payload.notes: Vendor: Roche FastStart Taq DNA Polymerase, 5 U/uL (Cat. 12 032 902 001 / 929 / 937 / 945 / 953). Hot-start enzyme: inactive during setup, activated by the 95 C step. Reaction table read directly from the pack insert (Version 15, Content Version December 2021). Activation: 95 C for about 4 min (range 2 to 6 min). Per-cycle denaturation: minimum 30 sec at 95 C. Annealing temperature is primer-dependent and is NOT specified by the insert; the 55 C / 30 sec shown here is an editable starting point (set about 5 C below the lower primer Tm). Extension at 72 C, 1 min/kb for fragments up to 3 kb; for fragments over 3 kb use 68 C. Cycle number is template-dependent (insert Results used 30 to 40 cycles). Final extension is conventional and not separately specified by the insert. Mg2+ optimal 1 to 4 mM (2 mM standard); titrate with Vial 4 if needed, or use the without-MgCl2 buffer (Vial 3). dNTP 100 to 500 uM each (200 uM standard); alternatively use 1 uL of 10 mM PCR Nucleotide Mix. Primers 0.2 to 0.5 uM (0.2 uM standard). Template 10 pg to 500 ng genomic, 10 pg to 100 ng plasmid or cDNA. For GC-rich or difficult templates add 10 uL GC-RICH solution (Vial 5) per 50 uL assay. For carryover prevention substitute 200 uM dTTP with 600 uM dUTP plus heat-labile Uracil-DNA Glycosylase. Source: Roche / Sigma-Aldrich FastStart Taq DNA Polymerase pack insert (ftaq-ro.pdf), transcribed directly from the PDF.
